persons.arguments_against: Strongest counter-case: there are 3 officials with this surname in the staff lists but only 1 biography, so the two stints could belong to a different McCaig (e.g. a relative also in Fiji service), and the 1913 stint begins as 'D. McCaig' with a single initial, which could be a different man than 'D. F. McCaig'. This fails: the 1913-1937 stint's department and grade progression (Postal Department clerk grades, then Senior Postal Clerk 1928-29, then Postmaster 1930-37) tracks the biography's printed career clause-for-clause (clk. 1912, Post-mstr. 1929, regraded postal clk. 1938), and the single-initial 'D. McCaig' rows (1913-15) sit inside the same continuous Postal Department record that becomes 'D. F. McCaig' by 1917 with no break. The 1950-1956 stint matches the biography's distinctive late-career switch to 'supt. prisons, 1947' and co-occurs with the biography in 6 edition-years; a second D. F. McCaig who was also Superintendent of Prisons in Fiji at exactly the years the biography claims is not a realistic hypothesis. The 1938-1949 staff-list gap is explained by the biography itself: 'on mil. serv. 1915-19, 1941-45, lt.-col.' and the 1938 regrading.
persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second D. F. McCaig in Fiji service in the same period (e.g. a staff-list row showing two McCaigs concurrently in different departments, or a forename for the early 'D. McCaig' rows inconsistent with Devon Francis), or a death/retirement record for Devon Francis McCaig before 1950.

notes: The two stints are almost certainly one continuous career split by the 1938 regrading and 1941-45 military service. Position progression, colony, initials, dates, and edition co-occurrence all agree; no live counter-hypothesis.
